source.title: BSEE Accident Investigation Report - Structural Damage / Crane Other Lifting

actions_taken: Compressor mechanic transported to medical facility
BSEE Lafayette District conducted onsite investigation on 2018-02-20
Physician treating injury as lumbar strain with re-evaluation planned
event.summary: On 2018-02-02 at approximately 0750 hours, a compressor mechanic was injured while attempting to reposition from the top of a compressor to the compressor walkway during water jumper installation. The mechanic's boot contacted the toe-rail causing a forward fall. The middle section of the handrail had been removed and replaced with yellow plastic chain secured with tie wraps. When the mechanic grabbed the chain while falling, it broke, resulting in a 9-foot fall to the deck below. The mechanic landed on his feet but sustained a lumbar strain injury.
recommendations: Handrails should not be removed and replaced with yellow plastic chain during maintenance
Guardrails must be constructed with steel-welded materials capable of withstanding 250 pounds force without failure
Guardrails must have top edge barrier of 42 inches above walking/working surface and middle barrier at approximately 21 inches
Guardrails must be secured to prevent accidental displacement
Workers must use 3 points of contact when climbing or walking on elevated surfaces
Good housekeeping practices must be maintained on work skids
Job Safety Analysis (JSA) requirements must be followed

hazards.name: Cluttered work environment with poor footing

threats.name: Loss of handrail integrity due to removal and replacement with inadequate chain
Worker loss of balance or slip on toe-rail
Worker not using 3 points of contact while holding equipment
Cluttered deck and poor housekeeping reducing stability

consequences.name: Worker fall from height to lower deck
Lumbar strain injury requiring medical treatment

controls.name: Guardrail system with steel-welded materials
controls.barrier_role: Physical barrier to prevent fall from height
controls.lod_basis: Primary engineering control to prevent workers from falling to lower levels

supporting_text: The middle section of the handrail was removed and replaced with yellow plastic chain that was secured with tie wraps.
While falling forward, the CM grabbed the yellow chain. The subject chain broke causing the CM to fall approximately 9 feet to the deck below.
The lessee failed to meet the requirements as per 33 CFR 143.110, (b) of this section

controls.name: Fall Protection Policy - Guardrail specifications
controls.barrier_role: Administrative control specifying guardrail design and installation requirements
controls.lod_basis: Policy requirement for guardrail construction and maintenance standards

supporting_text: Lessee failed to follow the Fall Protection Policy which states Guardrails are erected to prevent workers from falling to lower levels.
Guardrails must be capable of withstanding without failure a force of 250 pounds.
Guardrails must have a top edge barrier of 42 inches above the walking/working surface and a middle barrier approximately 21 inches

controls.name: Job Safety Analysis (JSA) - 3 points of contact requirement
controls.barrier_role: Administrative control requiring safe work practices during climbing and positioning
controls.lod_basis: JSA mandated safe work practice to maintain stability while working at height

supporting_text: Lessee failed to follow the Job Safety Analysis (JSA) which states under the Recommendations to Eliminate or Reduce Potential Hazards: to use 3 points of contact and to practice good housekeeping.
At the time of the incident, the CM was not using 3 points of contact when holding a water jumper in both hands.
The JSA mandated the following: to use 3 points of contact and to practice good housekeeping. The following rules were not followed.

controls.name: Job Safety Analysis (JSA) - Good housekeeping requirement
controls.barrier_role: Administrative control requiring clean and organized work environment
controls.lod_basis: JSA requirement to maintain safe working conditions and prevent slips/trips

supporting_text: The skid was full of cluttered piping and had poor footing at best.

Lessee failed to follow the Job Safety Analysis (JSA) which states under the Recommendations to Eliminate or Reduce Potential Hazards: to use 3 points of contact and to practice good housekeeping. The following rules were not followed.

controls.name: Regulatory compliance with 33 CFR 143.110
controls.barrier_role: Regulatory requirement for guardrail and fall protection standards
controls.lod_basis: Federal regulation requiring guards and rails on unprotected perimeters of floor or deck areas

supporting_text: The lessee failed to meet the requirements as per 33 CFR 143.110, (b) of this section, and areas not normally occupied, unprotected perimeter of all floor or deck areas and openings shall be rimmed with guards and rails or wire mesh fence.

The guard rail or fence shall be at least 42 inches high. The two intermediate rails shall be so placed that the rails are approximately evenly spaced between the guard rail and the floor or deck area

notes.rules: JSON output only. mentioned fields must be evidence-based. Use null for unknown values.
